hawkish_phrases: upside risks around medium-term inflationary pressures have moved slightly higher since May
risk that this temporary increase in inflation could put additional upward pressure on the wage and price-setting process
the disinflationary process had slowed and the risk of inflation expectations feeding through to second-round effects had risen
a more prolonged period of policy restrictiveness was likely to be warranted
Services consumer price inflation has been broadly flat over recent months
dovish_phrases: A margin of slack is judged to have emerged in the economy
The labour market had slack and was deteriorating further
Underlying UK GDP growth has remained subdued
downside risks in coming years, namely: inflation below forecast, and activity weak or an increased risk of recession
Weak underlying employment growth in recent quarters
summary: The MPC voted 5-4 to cut Bank Rate by 25bp to 4%, with one dissenter preferring a 50bp cut and four preferring to hold, reflecting substantial hawkish dissent concerned about elevated inflation risks and slowing disinflation despite emerging slack in the economy.
